title: 1985 Denver Mini Classic, Boys 10 - 11, Overall Awards Ceremony

enrichment.summary: An announcer presides over the awards ceremony for the Boys 10-11 age group at the 1985 Denver Mini Classic cycling race, naming the top finishers and presenting medals, jerseys, and prizes to the podium riders. The overall race champion, Mike Constantino (number 33), receives the yellow jersey, silver champion hat, a medal, and a $35 gift certificate.
enrichment.speaker: other

transcript.text: We have number 34, Tim Haugen. In fourth place is number 37, Eric Owen. And Eric will receive
A medal as well. Hending out our awards is race director Bud Biggers. Now on to the awards
Blocks. In third place, we will have receiving a medal as well as flowers and a jersey from
Bicycle Factory. Number 35, Michael Haggerty. Evidently, Michael Haggerty isn't here. We'll
Be able to get his award to him later on. In second place, we have receiving a medal,
A jersey from the Bicycle Factory. Number 41, Mike Turley. Mike now making his way to the
Awards blocks where he will receive his awards. Now our race champion for the Denver Mini Classic,
Receiving the yellow overall jersey, race leader jersey, the silver champion hat, a medal,
And a $35 gift certificate from the Denver department store. Number 33, our race champion,
Mike Constantino. And Mike will also be racing in the Red Zinger Mini Classic, so we'll look
Forward to seeing his efforts in Boulder starting on the 15th. Mike now receiving his medal and
His prizes. And if we could get one final victory salute from everyone. Everybody, hands up.
The 10-11 boys age group. If you could hold your hands up just for a little bit more so we could
Get some photographs here. All right, one final round of applause for the boys, 10-11 age group.
In their efforts in the Denver Mini Classic, Jeff Beeler is now going to post the Meridian Park
Circuit Race results for the 12-13 girls. In the meantime, we're going to move on.
